calendar.matters:
Notice Of Rescheduled Hearing On Petition To Enforce Foreign Subpoena
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: LYFT, INC.'S PETITION TO ENFORCE FOREIGN SUBPOENA

Appearances: Timothy Nally of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, LLP (916.564.5400) for moving party Lyft, Inc.; Gianni Mascioli of Hangley Aronchick Segal Pudlin & Schiller (215.496.7059) for moving party Lyft, Inc.; Julie Schwartz of Perkins Coie (206.359.3840) for Reddit, Inc.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Lyft, Inc.'s petition to enforce foreign subpoena is denied. Even if Lyft satisfied its burden that the discovery it seeks is permitted by the lawful consent exception to the Stored Communications Act, an issue which the court does not decide, Lyft's petition to enforce the second subpoena served on respondent Reddit, Inc. was filed after the mandatory deadline to compel compliance in two respects. First, because the second subpoena seeks the same information as the first subpoena, the second subpoena is an impermissible untimely effort to avoid the mandatory deadline to enforce the first subpoena, which had expired prior to service of the second subpoena. Second, Lyft's petition to enforce the second subpoena was filed and served after the mandatory deadline for enforcement of that subpoena. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Jennifer Roux, CSR 11033 (T: 707.477.9599, E: concierge@steno.com)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER LYFT, INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)
